coverage.note: Qualitative mapping over the 13 core feature production files (path types, file/assert/condition modules). 'Covered' = at least one C# or plang test exercises the file's changed behaviour with a real oracle. The two listed files are reached by tests but the specific v5 review-driven edit in each is not — see findings 2 and 3.

findings.issue: Test FileModule_PlangBehaviour_UnchangedFromProgramPerspective has a single statement: Assert.That(true).IsTrue(). It verifies a literal, not the file module.
findings.impact: A named test claims to guard 'file module behaviour from the program's perspective' and guards nothing. Any regression there passes. Placebo in the branch's headline test file.
findings.suggestion: Delete it, or make it real — drive a file save/read round-trip through the Read/Save handlers and assert the bytes (HandlerShapeTests.ReadHandler_Delegates_To_PathReadText is the model).

findings.issue: codeanalyzer v2 N2 switched path.Equals/GetHashCode from OrdinalIgnoreCase to RootComparison. No test anywhere constructs two paths and compares them — grep for Equals/GetHashCode in PathTests returns nothing.
findings.impact: Reverting line 169 to OrdinalIgnoreCase, or deleting the Equals override, keeps the whole suite green. The N2 fix (Linux /srv/x vs /SRV/x distinctness) is completely unguarded against regression.
findings.suggestion: Add a PathEqualityTests: same absolute path -> equal + equal hash; case-variant paths -> on Linux not-equal + distinct hash, on Windows equal (gate on OperatingSystem.IsWindows()); cover the path.Equals(string) overload too.

findings.issue: codeanalyzer v2 N3 added the IBooleanResolvable branch to ResolveTruthy and the source comment claims 'assert %path% is true is thus correct'. AssertTests was not touched since the merge base and never passes a path (or any IBooleanResolvable) as the asserted value.
findings.impact: Delete the 'if (data.Value is IBooleanResolvable)' branch and a path falls through to IsTruthy(object), which returns true for any non-null object (Default.cs:159). 'assert %missing_path% is true' then wrongly passes — the exact F3 always-true bug — and no test goes red.
findings.suggestion: Add AssertTests: IsTrue of a Data wrapping a FilePath to an existing in-root file -> Success true; IsTrue of a FilePath to a missing file -> Success false with Error is AssertionError. Mirror for IsFalse.

findings.issue: The F3 fix (if %path% exists reflects actual existence) is exercised at the plang level only by ConditionFileExistsSubSteps, which creates the file first and tests the TRUE branch. No plang test covers a missing file -> inner block must not run. No plang .goal files were added for the whole feature.
findings.impact: Revert the F3 fix so 'if X exists' is always-true and ConditionFileExistsSubSteps still passes (the file exists; the inner block runs either way). The headline semantic fix has no plang regression guard for its failing side. C# (DefaultEvaluatorTests.IfExists_PathToMissingFile_IsFalse) covers it, so this is a plang-layer gap, not a total blind spot.
findings.suggestion: Add a sibling .goal: 'if not-here.txt exists' with an inner set %innerRan%='yes', then assert %innerRan% equals 'no'.

findings.issue: The '/ exists' section does 'check if file exists, write to %info%' then 'assert %info% is not null'. Post-F3, file.exists always returns a non-null path object regardless of whether the file exists.
findings.impact: The assertion passes for a present and an absent file — it verifies 'exists returned an object', not 'exists reported the right answer'. Pre-existing test, but the F3 change makes its weakness actively misleading.
findings.suggestion: Tighten to 'assert %info% is true', or read existence semantically.

findings.issue: PLangFileSystem_AndWrapperLayer_AbsentFromProductionAssembly probes one hard-coded type name (app.types.path.Default.PLangFileSystem) and its comment claims the assertion 'fails by design — an honest red'. The wrapper layer is actually gone (no PLangFileSystem class, no System.IO.Abstractions ref under PLang/), so the test is green, not red — the comment is stale.
findings.impact: Low today (wrapper genuinely absent), but the stale comment misleads the next reader, and a single-namespace probe would miss a wrapper reintroduced under any other namespace — unlike its assembly-wide sister test NoProductionType_References_IFile.
findings.suggestion: Correct the comment to state the wrapper is removed; scan by simple type name across the assembly like the IFile sister test.

summary: Clean rebuild reproduces the green numbers exactly — C# 2882/2882, plang 203/203/0 stale, no regressions. The path-polymorphism C# suite is genuinely strong where it counts (the PathSchemeContractTests contract base, the F3 two-branch oracle, the N1 denied-probe oracle, typed-error assertions). But six findings hold it back from approval: a vacuous Assert.That(true).IsTrue() test posing as a behaviour guard; and — the costly ones — both N2 (path.Equals/GetHashCode) and N3 (assert path-truthiness), the two review-driven v5 fixes, ship with zero test coverage, so reverting either keeps the suite green. The branch's headline F3 fix has no plang regression guard for its failing side. Verdict: needs-fixes. All six are small, mechanical to close — roughly three C# tests, one plang .goal, one deleted/rewritten test, one corrected comment.
